displayTitle.description: The main title of the Wikipedia article, corresponding to the primary subject.

hatnote.description: A disambiguation notice appearing at the very top of the article. If present, it must follow the exact format: 'For other uses, see [Topic (disambiguation)](/topic-disambiguation).'

lead.description: The opening introductory paragraphs of the article before the first section heading. The subject's name must be bolded on its first mention using **bold**. It must contain internal links utilizing the format [Visible Text](/slug), where the slug is completely lowercase, stripped of all accents and diacritics, and uses hyphens instead of spaces.

infobox.description: The summary information box typically found floating on the top right of a Wikipedia article.

image_search.description: A short, multilingual search term used to query Wikimedia Commons and retrieve a real photograph. Use the most effective language for the subject (e.g., Portuguese for Brazilian subjects, English for universal ones).

image_caption.description: The caption describing the infobox image. May contain internal links following the standard slug format.

fields.description: An array of key-value pairs representing the structured data rows inside the infobox.

value.description: The value for the property. Can include text and internal links following the standard slug format.

sections.description: The main body of the article divided into hierarchical sections and subsections.

title.description: The heading title of the section.

level.description: The heading level indicating structural hierarchy. Use 2 for major sections (corresponding to ##) and 3 for subsections (corresponding to ###).

content.description: The full paragraph text of the section. Must contain internal links using the format [Visible Text](/slug), where the slug is completely lowercase, stripped of all accents and diacritics, and uses hyphens instead of spaces.

images.description: A collection of images embedded within this specific section.

search.description: A specific Wikimedia Commons search term to fetch a visually relevant real photograph. Must use the most effective language for the query to return accurate real-world results.

caption.description: The encyclopedic caption describing the image. May contain internal links following the standard slug format.

float.description: The alignment of the image relative to the text. Images must alternate between left and right throughout the article.

references.description: The bibliography and citations section found at the bottom of the article, providing sources for the claims made in the text.

author.description: The author of the cited work, conventionally formatted (e.g., 'Lastname, Firstname' or 'Lastname, F. I.').

title.description: The title of the cited work.

publication.description: The name of the journal, magazine, newspaper, or publishing house.

pages.description: The page range or specific page number of the citation (e.g., '12-14', '201').

categories.description: The list of Wikipedia categories the article belongs to, structurally placed at the very bottom of the page.

label.description: The visible human-readable category name (e.g., 'Eventos de 2019 no Brasil').

slug.description: The internal link slug for the category. Must be strictly lowercase, accent-free, and hyphen-separated (e.g., '/categoria-eventos-de-2019-no-brasil').
